title: Power of attorney, living will, and medical directive
description: Practice understanding of power of attorney, living will, and medical directive, as well as understanding of importance of having them.
topic_path: Power of attorney, living will, and medical directive

assessment_items.question_content:
Your friend is abroad for a year and needs you to sell their car.  

**What document do you need to make this happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

An elderly relative wants to set up a document so you can manage everything for them if they become seriously ill or injured.

**Which document should they choose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You have a major surgery coming up and want your friend Jamie to make medical decisions for you if you can’t communicate at the time. You’re unsure or don’t have time to list your specific medical wishes, but you trust Jamie to act in your best interests.

**Which document should you create?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A *will* and a *living will* may sound similar, but they do completely different things.

**Which of these describes a living will?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: decides who gets your stuff after you're gone
names a guardian for your children
lists your medical wishes if you can't communicate
picks a person to handle your money and property

assessment_items.question_content:
In the Jehovah's Witness faith, people don't accept blood transfusions due to their beliefs. A Jehovah's Witness wants to write down this specific wish so that doctors know what to do if they can't speak for themselves.

**Which document should they create?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You're getting ready for major surgery and want to write down your specific medical wishes in case you can't speak for yourself. You know exactly what treatments you want and don't want, and you want to make sure everyone follows them.

**Which document should you create?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A family member has set up a durable power of attorney, but not a living will. They are in a hospital and are unable to communicate.

**What problem could arise?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: family members disagree on the care
a distant relative inherits their house

assessment_items.question_content:
A family member has set up a living will, but not a durable power of attorney. They are on life support as a result of a car accident. 

**What problem could arise?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: family members disagree on the care
a distant relative inherits their house

assessment_items.question_content:
Mary's parents gave her a general power of attorney (POA)  many years ago. They are both currently very sick and are not keeping up with their financial obligations. 

**What problem could arise?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mary's cannot pay her parents' bills with their money.
Mary's sister is not allowed to help Mary with their parents.
A distant relative inherits Mary's parents' home.

assessment_items.question_content:
Knowing how to best carry out your wishes means matching them to the right document.

**Match each sentence below with the right document.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: A medical directive, power of attorney, and living will each have different needs: one asks you to name a **person**, another needs a **list of instructions**, and one requires **both**.
If you are just naming a person to do certain things, or simply speak for you, then you need a **power of attorney** document.
If you have specific medical wishes, like not to be given certain medication, you would list those in a **living will**.
If you have specific medical wishes, but *also* wish to name a person to make decisions in your best interest, you need a **medical (or advanced) directive.**

**Sentence** | **Document** 
- | - 
I name you to manage my bank accounts and pay bills. | $\boxed{\text{power of attorney}}$
I prefer not to have any major surgery without my family's approval. | $\boxed{\text{medical directive}}$
I prefer treatments that avoid strong pain medications. | $\boxed{\text{living will}}$

left: I name you to manage my bank accounts and pay bills.
I prefer not to have any major surgery without my family's approval.
I prefer treatments that avoid strong pain medications.

assessment_items.question_content:
Knowing how to best carry out your wishes means matching them to the right document.

**Match each sentence below with the right document.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: A medical directive, power of attorney, and living will each have different needs: one asks you to name a **person**, another needs a **list of instructions**, and one requires **both**.
If you are just naming a person to do certain things, or simply speak for you, then you need a **power of attorney** document.
If you have specific medical wishes, like what medications you want or don't want to take, you would list those decisions in a **living will**.
If you have specific medical wishes, but *also* wish to name a person to make decisions in your best interest, you need a **medical (or advanced) directive.**

**Sentence** | **Document** 
- | - 
I do not want feeding tubes and name my brother Jose to make any other medical decisions. | $\boxed{\text{medical directive}}$
I want to give my brother Jose access to my bank account.| $\boxed{\text{power of attorney}}$
I prefer to not be given medications tested on animals.  | $\boxed{\text{living will}}$

left: I do not want feeding tubes and name my brother Jose to make any other medical decisions.
I want to give my brother Jose access to my bank account.
I prefer to not be given medications tested on animals.

assessment_items.question_content:
Knowing how to best carry out your wishes means matching them to the right document.

**Match each sentence below with the right document.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: A medical directive, power of attorney, and living will each have different needs: one asks you to name a **person**, another needs a **list of instructions**, and one requires **both**.
If you just want control over who can control your finances, then you need a **power of attorney** document.
If you have specific medical wishes, like avoiding anesthesia, you would list those in a **living will**.
If you have specific medical wishes, but *also* wish to name a person to make decisions in your best interest, you need a **medical (or advanced) directive.**

**Sentence** | **Document** 
- | - 
I want to be conscious during all medical procedures, if possible. | $\boxed{\text{living will}}$
Please do not operate robotically, and consult my dad for any treatment.  | $\boxed{\text{medical directive}}$
Bo is the only person that can make financial decisions for me.   | $\boxed{\text{power of attorney}}$

left: I want to be conscious during all medical procedures, if possible.
Please do not operate robotically, and consult my dad for any treatment.
Bo is the only person that can make financial decisions for me.
